primary_speaker: Dr. Delmar (guest lecturer; identification inferred from internal reference at p57)
secondary_speakers: Thomas W. Eagar (interjections at p22–p23, p56–p58)
topic_summary: Safety factor in design when no governing code exists. Guest lecturer Dr. Delmar presents two case studies from his own engineering practice: (1) a homemade utility trailer he designed and built, with attention to placing welds in compression rather than tension to manage uncertainty in weld quality at points of highest tensile stress; (2) an ATV/quad front suspension with control arms, illustrating how worst-case load analysis, field testing with force transducers, and service history together let manufacturers reduce safety factors toward unity. Closes with a digression on a nuclear facility where a contractor drilled unauthorized holes in seismic-designed structural members, and the non-obvious remedy (do NOT plug-weld; add a stiffener away from the strain zone). Tom Eagar interjects to anchor the discussion in the structural welding code (50% of yield for members, 30% for welds; 1.67 safety factor for buildings, 2.0 for bridges) and closes with a Florida tree-stand fatality caused by weld placement.
key_cases: Delmar's homemade utility trailer (8'×4', single axle, 1000 lb/wheel)
Storage-garage car elevator failed when loaded eccentrically by a forklift
ATV/quad front suspension control arm design
Nuclear facility seismic-frame members drilled for electrical conduit (post-hoc remediation)
Florida tree-stand failure (Tom's anecdote — weld in tension killed a user)
key_figures_referenced: Structural welding code: design allowable stress = 50% of minimum yield for members, 30% for welds
Safety factor 1.67 for buildings, 2.0 for bridges
S-N curve for steel: ~50% of yield gives infinite life
25 lb/ft² wind pressure assumption at 65 mph
Friction factor 0.6–0.7 for rubber on road
notes: Anomalous lecture in the Eagar corpus: Tom is largely silent and a guest lectures. Speaker attribution 'Dr. Delmar' inferred from Tom's reference at p57. Several captioner garbles flagged for editorial review (see transformation_log.md). Lecture recording appears truncated mid-sentence at the end.
